author: andrew-k-park
title: benchmark_app crashes with STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION in sync mode with -no_warmup on Windows

failed_jobs.name: Samples
failed_jobs.symptom: test_benchmark_app_no_warmup_with_api_modes[sync-CPU-Python] - benchmark_app returned exit code 3221225477 (STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION) after completing all 11 inference steps

key_errors: FAILED tests/smoke_tests/test_benchmark_app.py::test_benchmark_app_no_warmup_with_api_modes[sync-CPU-Python] - subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '('benchmark_app', '-i', ..., '-d', 'CPU', '-niter', '1', '-nireq', '1', '-api', 'sync', '-no_warmup')' returned non-zero exit status 3221225477.
benchmark_app -i dog-224x224.bmp -m bvlcalexnet-12.onnx -d CPU -niter 1 -nireq 1 -api sync -no_warmup returned 3221225477
root_cause: The Python benchmark_app completed all 11 inference steps and printed the statistics report successfully, then crashed during Python process cleanup/shutdown with exit code 3221225477 (0xC0000005 = Windows STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION). The inference itself completed normally. This is a pre-existing flaky crash that occurs intermittently in Windows benchmark_app teardown, unrelated to PR #36738 which only modified GPU plugin format-selection logic. A similar crash was previously observed on 2026-06-29 with the async variant of the same test (pattern 2fe5fca9503d7326).
reproduction_steps: Run on Windows: benchmark_app -i dog-224x224.bmp -m bvlcalexnet-12.onnx -d CPU -niter 1 -nireq 1 -api sync -no_warmup
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed Samples job as this is a transient crash in Python process teardown
Investigate benchmark_app Python teardown for use-after-free / double-free in the -no_warmup code path on Windows
Check if the crash is related to TBB thread pool teardown during benchmark_app exit
Consider adding a test skip or retry for this flaky test until the underlying crash is fixed
prevention_strategies: The benchmark_app Python wrapper should guard against access violations during process teardown. Consider adding crash handling or a skip marker for the known-flaky -no_warmup combinations on Windows.
historical_context: A similar crash (STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION / exit code 3221225477) was observed on 2026-06-29 in run 28260319677 for the async variant test_benchmark_app_no_warmup_with_api_modes[async-CPU-Python] (pattern hash 2fe5fca9503d7326, count=1). Both sync and async modes fail with the same exit code, suggesting a shared teardown code path.
